channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Noongar mother Mary Ann Miller dies after being evicted, struggling with domestic violence and inadequate support
item.description: Mary Ann Miller, a Noongar woman and mother of seven, tragically passed away in March 2026 due to sepsis, just two weeks after giving birth. Her death has sparked concerns about the lack of support fo...

item.title: Dawn Zulueta Reveals Desired Role for Potential Acting Comeback
item.description: Veteran actress Dawn Zulueta recently opened up about her potential return to acting, sharing the type of role she would like to take on. While she has been less active in showbiz in recent years, Zul...

item.title: Coalition proposes doubling Australia’s fuel reserves to 60 days to boost national security
item.description: The coalition in Australia has unveiled a plan to more than double the nation's fuel reserves to 60 days to strengthen supply chains amid global uncertainty. Opposition Leader Angus Taylor and Nationa...

item.title: Tinubu Outlines Responsibilities For Newly Appointed Ambassadors
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don talk say the newly appointed Nigerian ambassadors get important work to do. Through the Secretary to the Government of the Federation, George Akume, Tinubu yan say their main...

item.title: Ontario education minister faces criticism for questioning stakeholders during Bill 101 hearings
item.description: Ontario’s Education Minister Paul Calandra is facing criticism after directly questioning union representatives during legislative hearings on Bill 101, a proposed law that would significantly alter t...

item.title: Garissa Political Aspirant Arrested for Inciting Violence Following Tseikuru Attack
item.description: A female political aspirant from Garissa County has been arrested in connection with the deadly attack in Tseikuru, Kitui County, that left seven people dead. The arrest followed the circulation of a ...

item.title: Hamilton Community Mourns Teen Shooting Victim, Calls for Action
item.description: The Hamilton community is in mourning following the shooting death of a 16-year-old boy inside a local mall. The incident has prompted widespread grief, a call for action to address youth violence, an...

item.title: Ben&Ben, HORI7ON, and ASEAN Artists Highlight Day 2 of 2026 ROUND Festival in the Philippines
item.description: Day 2 of the 2026 ROUND Festival in the Philippines featured a vibrant lineup of local and regional artists, with Ben&Ben, HORI7ON, and South Korean act 10CM leading the performances. The event showca...

item.title: Florida Governor DeSantis Unveils Controversial Partisan Congressional Map
item.description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis unveiled a new congressional map ahead of the 2026 elections, showing a potential gain of four seats for Republicans. The map, released directly to Fox News before submis...

item.title: Scholarship program helps Kai Buchanan pursue university education
item.description: Kai Buchanan, originally unsure about pursuing university after high school, changed his mind after receiving the Snow Scholarship from the University of Canberra. The program is designed to support s...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee vs Suvendu Adhikari looms in Bhowanipore as Bengal’s key assembly face‑off
item.description: The upcoming West Bengal assembly election is expected to see a major showdown in the Bhowanipore constituency of south Kolkata, where Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee of the Trinamool Congress will fac...

item.title: Britain introduces law banning cigarette sales for future generations
item.description: Britain is set to implement a groundbreaking law under the Tobacco and Vapes Bill that will prevent anyone born on or after January 1, 2009, from ever purchasing cigarettes. This rolling age restricti...

item.title: Nigeria Football Federation mourns the passing of Michael Eneramo
item.description: The Nigeria Football Federation (NFF) don announce say dem dey very shaken as former Super Eagles striker, Michael Eneramo, don die after e collapse during one friendly match for Kaduna on Friday morn...

item.title: Lindsay Hoyle approves debate on Starmer referral to privileges committee
item.description: The Speaker of the House of Commons, Lindsay Hoyle, has unexpectedly approved a debate concerning the potential referral of Prime Minister Keir Starmer to the privileges committee over the appointment...

item.title: Alexandre Boulerice Announces Departure from Federal Politics to Join Québec Solidaire in Gouin
item.description: Alexandre Boulerice, a prominent figure in Canadian federal politics, has announced he will step down from his current position and join the Québec Solidaire party to run for a seat in the Gouin ridin...

item.title: Augustus Muli Pledges to Hold Leaders Accountable Over Ukambani’s Development
item.description: Kitui – Augustus Muli, leader of the National Liberal Party (NLP), has promised to hold accountable any leader who undermines the development of Ukambani. Speaking after comments on former Deputy Pres...

item.title: Bongo Flava Artist Spack Passes Away at Home, Fans Pay Tribute
item.description: Tanzanian Bongo Flava musician Spack has died at his home in Chanika, leaving fans and neighbours in deep sorrow. According to reports, he was with his children before retiring to his room for the nig...

item.title: The Impact of Apartheid on Today's American Reality
item.description: In this thought-provoking piece, Stephanie Urdang reflects on her upbringing in apartheid South Africa and how it shaped her perception of modern-day America. She draws striking parallels between the ...

item.title: Suspect in Constance Githinji’s Death Linked to Previous Assault Case
item.description: The tragic death of 22-year-old Murang’a University student Constance Githinji has taken a new turn. Constance fell from the sixth floor of a short-stay apartment in Kileleshwa, Nairobi, on 27th April...

item.title: UNICEF Raises Alarm Over Worsening Child Crisis in Central Sahel Amid Violence and Displacement
item.description: UNICEF don raise serious concern say about 7.5 million children for Central Sahel region—covering Niger, Burkina Faso and Mali—dey in urgent need of humanitarian support. According to Deputy Executive...
